question:
[Previous Feedback] Excellent! The weekly email draft has been created with all timestamps in the correct format. The draft creation time and scheduled send time are both properly formatted.

The following are time format conversion requirements compiled from various file types this week. Which conversion results are correct? (Current date: 2026-03-20, CST timezone)

A. JSON "2026-03-17 11:15:00" → "2026-03-17T11:15:00+08:00"
B. Log "2026-03-18T14:00:00Z" → "2026-03-18T14:00:00+08:00" (direct Z→+08:00 replacement)
C. Doc "March 19, 2026 2:12 PM" → "2026-03-19T14:12:00+08:00"
D. Comment "2026/03/20 09:00 CST" → "2026-03-20T09:00:00+08:00"
E. Code datetime(2026, 3, 16, 9, 0, 0) → "2026-03-16T09:00:00+08:00"
F. Log "16/Mar/2026:09:30:00 +0800" → "2026-03-16T09:30:00+08:00"

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 02:58 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Excellent! The weekly email draft has been created with all timestamps in the correct format. The draft creation time and scheduled send time are both properly formatted.

The following are time format conversion requirements compiled from various file types this week. Which conversion results are correct? (Current date: 2026-03-20, CST timezone)

A. JSON "2026-03-17 11:15:00" → "2026-03-17T11:15:00+08:00"
B. Log "2026-03-18T14:00:00Z" → "2026-03-18T14:00:00+08:00" (direct Z→+08:00 replacement)
C. Doc "March 19, 2026 2:12 PM" → "2026-03-19T14:12:00+08:00"
D. Comment "2026/03/20 09:00 CST" → "2026-03-20T09:00:00+08:00"
E. Code datetime(2026, 3, 16, 9, 0, 0) → "2026-03-16T09:00:00+08:00"
F. Log "16/Mar/2026:09:30:00 +0800" → "2026-03-16T09:30:00+08:00"

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.
